THIS IS A DELAYED REPORT FROM LATE SATURDAY NIGHT/EARLY SUNDAY MORNING. KARNES COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT REPORTS QUITE A BIT OF TREE DAMAGE AND UPROOTING OF TREES OCCURRED ALONG FARM ROAD 99 SOUTHWEST OF KARNES CITY AND KENEDY. KENEDY AIRPORT DID REPORT A WIND GUST OF 50 MPH WITH THIS CLUSTER OF STORMS. SOME OF THE WIND DAMAGE EXTENDS EAST TOWARD RUNGE. APPEARS THE DAMAGE IS FAIRLY WIDESPREAD AND CONSISTENT WITH MICROBURST WINDS AS THE CLUSTER OF STORMS MOVED EAST ACROSS THE COUNTY BETWEEN 1 AND 2 AM EARLY SUNDAY MORNING.

*** 1 INJ *** NWS STORM SURVEY TEAM CONCLUDED THAT AN EF1 TORNADO WITH ESTIMATED PEAK WINDS OF 100 MPH BEGAN JUST TO THE WEST OF CR 234 AND NORTH OF CR 239 TO THE SOUTHWEST OF JARRELL. THE TORNADO TRACKED EASTWARD AND DISSIPATED TO THE EAST OF LITTLE ROAD. PLEASE SEE THE NWS DAMAGE SURVEY PUBLIC INFORMATION STATEMENT FOR THE 10/24/2022 JARRELL TORNADO EVENT FOR ADDITIONAL DETAILS.
TDEM DISTRICT COMMAND REPORTS TRAILERS BLOCKING THE I-35 SERVICE ROAD IN JARRELL. ALSO REPORTS WINDOWS BLOWN OUT AT THE JARRELL FIRE STATION. REPORT TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.

NUMEROUS HOMES AND BUSINESSES SUSTAINED MINOR ROOF DAMAGE EAST OF I-35, BOTH NORTH AND SOUTH OF CR-313. A FIRE STATION AND A BUSINESS ALSO HAD ITS GARAGE DOORS BLOWN OUT. ALL DAMAGE WAS IN A ROUGHLY WEST TO EAST ORIENTATION. THE NWS STORM SURVEY TEAM CONCLUDED THAT THE DAMAGE WAS MOST LIKELY CAUSED BY A LARGE SWATH OF 60-80 MPH STRAIGHT-LINE WINDS. TIME ESTIMATED VIA RADAR.
